rubric_name: Generative AI Full-Stack Engineer (GenAI + RAG + Full-Stack Ownership) Rubric

jd.jd_summary: Own the full stack of an end-to-end AI recruitment platform: React dashboards, FastAPI services, Python AI pipelines, RAG with vector search/hybrid search, and production deployment.
core_responsibilities: Build React dashboards with filters, analytics, and candidate insights
Develop FastAPI backend services including API endpoints, report generation, and data management
Implement Python cron/worker jobs to fetch CVs, run LLM scoring, and generate candidate reports
Design and ship production RAG pipelines (chunking, embeddings, vector search, reranking, grounding)
Model and operate Supabase/PostgreSQL with pgvector for hybrid search
Integrate external systems (LLM providers, workforce platforms, email delivery) and deploy to cloud
must_haves_from_jd: Bachelor’s in CS/Software Engineering/AI or related
1–4 years building production GenAI/RAG systems; ability to build complete apps end-to-end
Python + JavaScript/React; FastAPI backend; React frontend
Real-world RAG expertise: chunking, embeddings, vector search, reranking, grounding
DevOps: Git, REST design, testing, error handling, cron scheduling, cloud deployments
External API integrations (LLM providers, email services)
nice_to_haves_from_jd: MCP (Model Context Protocol) or AI agent frameworks

compliance_requirements: Work onsite in Ara Damansara, Petaling Jaya (onsite role)
Bachelor’s degree in CS/Software Engineering/AI or related field (required)

must_have.requirement: End-to-end full-stack ownership: deliver complete features from React UI through FastAPI services, database, and AI/RAG pipeline to production deployment.
evidence_signals: Shipped a production web app with both frontend and backend components owned end-to-end
Can describe architecture decisions and tradeoffs across UI, APIs, DB, and background jobs
Has operated/maintained a production system post-launch (bug fixes, incidents, iteration)
negative_signals: Only worked on isolated tickets with no end-to-end ownership
Cannot explain how components integrate or how data flows across the stack
must_have.implementation_note: Score higher when candidate provides concrete system diagrams, PRs, or deployments showing ownership across layers.

must_have.requirement: Strong Python proficiency for backend logic and AI processing jobs (data ingestion, scoring workflows, report generation).
evidence_signals: Built Python services or workers handling real data pipelines and external API calls
Uses robust patterns: typed models, structured logging, retries/backoff, idempotency
Demonstrates performance and reliability considerations (batching, rate limits, caching)
negative_signals: Primarily scripting without production reliability practices
Struggles to reason about failures, retries, rate limits, or data consistency

must_have.requirement: React.js frontend development for recruiter/hiring-manager dashboards with filters, analytics, and candidate insights UX.
evidence_signals: Built production React dashboards with complex state, tables/filters, and data fetching
Can discuss UI performance (memoization, pagination/virtualization) and accessibility
Uses component patterns and clean separation of concerns (hooks, state management)
negative_signals: Only minor UI edits; no experience building data-heavy dashboards
Cannot explain how to structure components/state for maintainability

must_have.requirement: FastAPI backend services: well-designed REST endpoints, auth/data access patterns, and report generation APIs.
evidence_signals: Implemented FastAPI (or comparable Python API framework) in production
Can describe API versioning, validation (Pydantic), pagination, and error contracts
Experience generating/exporting reports (PDF/HTML/JSON) or structured outputs
negative_signals: No experience designing APIs; only consumed APIs
must_have.implementation_note: FastAPI is preferred; comparable frameworks acceptable if candidate demonstrates strong API design fundamentals.

must_have.requirement: Production RAG implementation: chunking strategies, embeddings, vector retrieval, reranking, and grounding for candidate–job fit analysis.
evidence_signals: Has built and shipped a RAG pipeline used by real users or stakeholders
Can justify chunking choices and retrieval settings; understands grounding and citations
Has used reranking or retrieval tuning to improve relevance (offline eval or A/B learnings)
negative_signals: Only toy/demo RAG projects with no real data constraints
Cannot explain failure modes (hallucinations, drift, low recall) or mitigations
must_have.implementation_note: Score highest for candidates who can show evaluation results, retrieval debugging, and mitigation strategies.

must_have.requirement: Database & search: Supabase/PostgreSQL with pgvector (or equivalent) implementing hybrid search (keyword + semantic) and data modeling.
evidence_signals: Designed Postgres schemas and indexes; understands query plans and performance basics
Implemented vector similarity search with pgvector (or Pinecone/Weaviate) and hybrid retrieval
Can describe data lifecycle: ingestion, normalization, deduplication, and backfills/migrations
negative_signals: No hands-on experience with Postgres data modeling or indexing
Treats vector search as a black box; cannot explain hybrid retrieval
must_have.implementation_note: Accept Pinecone/Weaviate experience if candidate can translate concepts to pgvector/Postgres.

must_have.requirement: LLM integration: Claude/OpenAI APIs, embeddings, prompt engineering, and safe/consistent output generation for scoring and reports.
evidence_signals: Integrated LLM APIs with robust rate-limit handling, retries, and cost controls
Uses prompt structuring and output schemas (JSON mode/function calling where applicable)
Understands safety considerations and reduces hallucination via grounding and constraints
negative_signals: Hardcodes prompts without evaluation or versioning
No awareness of cost/latency or provider limits

must_have.requirement: DevOps & deployment: Git workflows, cloud deployment (Render/Vercel/Railway/AWS/GCP), cron scheduling, and environment management.
evidence_signals: Has deployed and monitored services in at least one cloud environment
Can set up cron/queues/workers and manage secrets/config across environments
Understands rollback strategies and basic observability (logs/metrics/alerts)
negative_signals: No production deployment experience; only local development
Cannot explain env separation, secrets management, or deployment pipelines

must_have.requirement: Engineering quality: testing, error handling, and resilient integrations for external APIs (LLM providers, email services, workforce platforms).
evidence_signals: Writes meaningful tests for API endpoints, critical business logic, and RAG components
Implements timeouts, retries/backoff, dead-letter patterns, and graceful degradation
Can describe incident learnings and how reliability was improved
negative_signals: Little/no testing; relies on manual verification
Ignores failure handling for third-party APIs
must_have.implementation_note: Even at 1–4 years experience, prioritize candidates demonstrating strong reliability instincts.

nice_to_have.skill: LangChain experience for composing RAG chains/tools and productionizing retrieval workflows
LlamaIndex experience for document ingestion/indexing and retrieval abstractions
MCP (Model Context Protocol) or AI agent frameworks (tool use, orchestration, guardrails)
CI/CD pipelines for automated testing and deployment (GitHub Actions/GitLab CI/etc.)
Docker for containerized local development and consistent deploy environments
RAG evaluation/monitoring practices (retrieval metrics, golden sets, prompt/versioning)

scoring.weights_policy: Must-have requirements sum to 90 points; nice-to-have skills sum to 10 points. Compliance is pass/fail gating.

score_interpretation.label: Exceptional
score_interpretation.guidance: Strong end-to-end ownership with proven production RAG + full-stack delivery; minimal ramp time.

score_interpretation.label: Strong
score_interpretation.guidance: Meets core stack with solid RAG and deployment experience; some gaps are coachable.

score_interpretation.label: Borderline
score_interpretation.guidance: Partial match; may lack production RAG depth or true end-to-end ownership.

score_interpretation.label: Not a fit
score_interpretation.guidance: Missing key must-haves or cannot demonstrate production-ready practices.

principles: Score only on evidence tied to requirements; ignore pedigree signals unrelated to job performance.
Do not penalize candidates for non-traditional backgrounds if they meet compliance and can show equivalent capability.
Use consistent questioning across candidates for RAG, deployments, and end-to-end ownership.
prohibited_signals: University ranking prestige as a proxy for capability
Accent, nationality, age, gender, marital/parental status
Salary history or unrelated personal circumstances
structured_interview_prompts: Ask for a concrete RAG system they shipped: data sources, chunking, retrieval, reranking, grounding, evaluation.
Ask for an end-to-end feature walkthrough: UI -> API -> DB -> background jobs -> deployment/monitoring.
Ask about one production incident or failure mode and how they improved reliability.

include_sections: Must-have scoring table with evidence and risks
Overall recommendation and interview focus areas
report_format.evidence_requirement: Each scored item must cite at least one concrete artifact (project, metric, PR, deployment, architecture explanation) or interview demonstration.
